content.full_text:
CalifOrnia
Fair Political
Practices Commission
October 4, l99L
Ms. Diane M. Fishburn
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL,
FONG & LEIDIGH
3oo capitof MalL, Suite 350
Sacranento, CA 95814
Re: Your Request for Infornal Advice
Our FiIe No. I-91-3 9 7
Dear Ms. Fishburn:
we have received your I'etter dated August 16, 1991, in- which
you seek advice on behilf of Carmen Sabatino regarding the I'sender
identification" 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
rrtr"1rr 1 . 1
As Iilr. Sabatino's authorized representative we provide you
with advice Pursuant to Regulation 18329, but as your request does
not referencl a pending goiernnental decision, the advice provided
is informal only.2 th6 iollowing advice is based upon the facts
pi""ia"a in youi retter of Augusi 16, -1991, and suppremented by
[etepnone coiversations with your office on August 22, August 28'
and October 1. 1991 .
OUESTTON
Is Mr. sabatino's publication, rtcheck and Ba1ance, " subject
to the Act's rtsender identification" 
requirernents?
CONCLUS ION
As a mass mailing sent by a candidate or a conmittee' the
publication "Check and Bal'ance" is subject to the sender
identification requirenents of the Act.
1 co.r.rr,..nt code sections SLooo-91015. Alr statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated' 
Commission
reguJ.ations appear at 2 california code of Regulatj'ons. section
ieooo, et seq-.- A1I references to regulations are to Title 2'
oivision e or the California code of Regulations'
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
i^.""ity 
provided by an opinion or formal written advice'
(Section 8311.4; Regulation 18329 (c) . )
street, suite 8oo . P.O. Box 80? o Sacramento cA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322'5660
4z8l


File No. I-9■-397
Page 2
FACTS
carmen Sabatino is a candidate for the office of mayor for
the city of Modesto in the Novenber 1991 election. 
Mr. Sabatino
founded carino Publications through which he publishes 'rCheck and
Balancer on a rnonthly or bimonthly basis. You have indicated that
the Dublication addresses local issues, government news, and
eleclions, and provides litr. Sabatino with a forum in which to
editorialize on these toPics.
CheckandBalancehasaPaidsubscriptionlistofbet$'een3oo
and 5oo, is distributed free of charge to individuals and groups,
and is available for purchase at tvo local bookstores (recently,
ii ""pi"= were sold at ttre bookstores during a one month period) '
You hive indicated that Mr. Sabatino expects to include
infornation relating to the forthcoming nunicipal election,
including endorsing his own candidacy and discussing the
candidacies of others for other municipal offices. 
We note that
when Mr. Sabatino unsuccessfuJ.Iy ran for Assembly in 1990, check
and BaLance prominently featured his candidacy and urged readers
to support hirn in the eLection.
FinalLy,youhaveindicatedthatcheckandBalance,forrnedin
1988, is inlendea to be an ongoing enterprise, continuing after
the November, 1991 election, ina €frat there are presently no plans
io after the publicatj'on's forrnat or distribution' 
You have also
indicated that Ur. Sabatino does not use funds of his carnpaign
conrnittee to publish or distribute Check and Balance'
ANALYS I S
Governnent code Section 84305 provides that a candidate or
comrnittee which sends a mass nailing must include the rrname'
street address, and city of the candidate or comnittee on the
;;l;i;" 
or """n piece oi mair in the mass mailing and. on at.reast
one of the inserts ..--" 
(section 84305(a)') !''hen the sender of
the nass nailing is a single candidate or conmittee,
identification ot the nane, street address, and city of the
"i"aiait. 
or connittee is required only on the outside of each
piece of rnail. 
(Section 84305(b).) When the sender of a mass
mailing is a controlled committee, the name of the person
controiling the comnittee is to be included ln addition to the
information required by subdivisj'on (a) '
A rrmass mailing'r is over two hundred substantiatly simi)'ar
piecei of nail sent in a calendar nonth. (Section 82041'5') The
i'sender" of a rnass mailing is defined as the candidate or
cornmittee paying for the largest po5tion of reportable
""pE"Jit".^"i'atiributed 
to the deiign, printing, and posting of
the rnailing. 
(Regulation 18435 (b) . )


FiIe No. I-9L-397
Page 3
Requlation 18225
You have suggested that check and Balance falls within the
'reditorial exception" of Regulation 18225(b) (4) (A), thus exenpting
the publication from the sender identification requirement, and
have referred to the commission's opinion in In Re Christiansen
(Goleta vallev Todav), 1 FPPC Ops' 170 (L975), to support this
suggestion.
An exception to the definition of "expenditure" applies to
those costs incurred by a "regularly published newspaper, maqazine
or other periodical of general circulation" routinely providing
news, articLes, and general interest commentary. (Regulation
1822s (b) (4) (A) . )
Check and Balance, because it is publ j.shed and distributed by
a candidate for office, does not qualify for this exception.
Regulation L8225(a) (2) provides that an expenditure is a payment
made by a candidate unless it is clear that the payment was made
for personal purposes unrelated to his or her candidacy or status
as an of f iceholder.
The definition of 'texpenditure" in Regulation 18225(b) and
ihe e:.:ceplions to the definition as provided in subsection (b) (4)
specificllly 
exctude expenditures made by candidates. Regulation
lb225(b) stltes that the def ini.tj.on of expenditure is applicabJ-e
to a payment made "by any person, other than those persons ...
describld in subsection (a) ... " candidates are included in
subsection (a). Therefore. the 'reditorial exceptionrr as provided
for in subsection (b) (4) (A) is not applicable to check and Balance
because the expenditures made for the publication are made by a
candidate.
When Mr. Sabatino makes a payment/ expend iture for a purpose
related to his candidacy, the Payment or expenditure is made for a
political purpose. (Regulation 18225(a). ) In prevj.ously
iesponding to a candidate who produced and distributed his own
nerspaper, the conmission has indicated that when the publication
was used as a means of supportj.ng his candidacy, the costs
incurred in connection with its publication and distribution were
reportable and discloseable. 
(warren Advice Letter, A-86-l'20')
Because no exceptions as provided by subsection (b) are
applicable, thi! would also appear to be the case with respect to
check and Balance. The sender identification requirements of
Sectj-on 84305 would therefore be applicable to Mr- Sabatino's
publication at least subsequent to the tine when he is a candidate
within the meaning of Section 82007.J
3 Und.. rule ennunci.ated in Regulation 18531.5, because I'1r '
Sabatino directs and controls the decisions of his canpaign
committee and Carino Publications, the comnj'ttee and Carino
Publj,cations are considered one entity for purposes of
expenditures that support his candidacy or support or oppose other
candidates for elective office or positions on ballot measures'


File No. I-9L-397
Page 4
First Amendment considerations
Section 843o5 does not prohibit Mr. Sabatino from publishing
his newsletter nor interfere lrith his ability to express his views
on an election campaign. Section 84305 only requires a specific
sender identification that lIr. Sabatino has already essentially
provided elsewhere in the publication itself.
Your reference to Schuster v. Municipal Court (L980) 109
Cat.App.3d 887) does not change thj.s conclusion. In Schuster, the
appetllte court invalidated forner Elections code section 2941o,
which in part provided that
(a) Every person . .. is guiltY of a
nisdeneanor who causes to be reproduced ...
any ... matter having reference to an
election, to any candidate, or to any measure,
or causes such reproduced natter to be posted
or distributed, unless there appears ... the
name and address of the business or residence
of a person responsible for it.
The court invalidated this section as an overbroad
infringement of the constitutional right to distribute anony:nous
literafure. 
(Schuster, supra, 109 cal.App.3d at 894.) The basis
for this holding, however, uas that the statute scrutinized by the
court prohibited all anonlrmous literature of any kind. . (Schuster,
supra, 109 Cat.APp.3d at 891.) The application of Section 84305
is not comparable in scope to the statute invalidated in schuster.
Finalty, we wish to note that in the copies of Check and
Balance that you have provided, Mr. sabatino prorninently discloses
his name as the pubJ.isher, as well. as the name and address of the
publishing company. As such, check and Balance is not the type of
rranonymous publicationrr at issue in Schuster that wilI, as you
suggest, sonehow be silenced if the sender identification
requj-rements of Section 84305 are imposed. The necessary
information is already be j.ng provided within the publication.
AII that is required by Section 84305 is that the nane,
street address, and city of the candidate or comtnittee sending the
mass mai.ling be placed on the outside of each piece of mail in the
mass mail.ing, and perhaps on at least one of the inserts. 
When
the sender of a nass rnailing is a controlled committee, the nane
of the person controlling the cornrnittee is also included. In
other $rords, information that Mr. Sabatino already provides in the
publication nust be provided one additional time on the outside of
the piece of mail.


File No. I-91-3 97
Page 5
Finally, we believe you mischaracterize the sender
identification as requiring identification ras carnpaign
literature." 
Application of Section 84305 does not create
campaign literatuie. 
under the facts as you have presented them,
we -belleve the recipients of Check and Balance who concfude that
the pubtication is campaign literature do so because of the
contLnt of the publication, and not because of section 84305'
I hope this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested-. Please contact ure if you have any further questions
regarding this :natter at (916) 322-59Ot.
Sincerely,
scott
Ha I Iabr in
General counsel
`藝
LegaI Division

」onathan

QUESTION: Is Mr. sabatino's publication, rtcheck and Ba1ance, " subject
to the Act's rtsender identification" 
requirernents?

CONCLUSION: As a mass mailing sent by a candidate or a conmittee' the
publication "Check and Bal'ance" is subject to the sender
identification requirenents of the Act.
